_comment.1: Open-vs-closed attenuation measurement of the tip/tilt loop.
_comment.2: This is steering_tuned.json (same camera, CoM, kalman, pid,
_comment.3: clamp, and piplate settings) with one extra device --
_comment.4: anyloop:attenuation_test -- inserted between the CoM and the
_comment.5: kalman/pid stages. It holds the loop OPEN for open_time
_comment.6: seconds (it zeroes the error feeding the kalman/pid, so the
_comment.7: integrator and line oscillators stay parked and the DAC sits
_comment.8: at its bias) while recording the raw CoM error, then lets the
_comment.9: loop CLOSE, waits settle_time seconds for acquisition and
_comment.10: kalman re-convergence (the loop notes say steady state needs
_comment.11: t > 25 s), records for closed_time seconds, then computes
_comment.12: Welch PSDs of both phases and writes attenuation.dat plus
_comment.13: attenuation.pdf: per-axis open/closed PSD overlays, the
_comment.14: closed/open ratio in dB vs frequency with the strongest
_comment.16: annotated, and the error variance contributed by each 5 Hz
_comment.17: band (open vs closed, top closed bands tagged with their %
_comment.18: of the total). Each phase's sample rate is measured from its
_comment.19: own timestamps, and the open PSD is re-gridded onto the
_comment.20: closed-phase frequency axis, so the closed loop running
_comment.21: slower (DAC writes) does not skew the frequencies. The loop
_comment.22: stops by itself when the test is done. EDIT open_time /
_comment.23: closed_time below to taste; the 2026-07-10 verification used
_comment.24: 120 s + 120 s, and the loop notes warn that runs under 60 s
_comment.25: are not trustworthy because the vibration environment is

pipeline._comment: Send raw frame to watch_steering.jl on port 64730
Track a 15x15 window centred on the previous CoM, init on the beam at frame centre (see steering_tuned.json notes).
Send [y, x] CoM vector to watch_steering.jl on port 64731
Send [y, x] CoM vector to watch_error_ts.jl on port 64732 (full rate)

pipeline._note1: THE TEST. Sits after the CoM sinks so the watchers still see the true error, and before the kalman so the open-phase recording is the raw error, not a prediction. start_delay covers the 2 s pid/piplate start_delay holds plus CoM window acquisition. settle_time 25 s matches the loop notes' steady-state threshold (pid acquisition + kalman 10 s handover ramp).
pipeline._note2: nfft 4096 at the ~2400 Hz loop rate gives ~0.6 Hz bins -- enough to resolve the 22.08 / 30.8 / 59.63 Hz lines. Sample rate is measured from timestamps, not assumed. Outputs land in the working directory: attenuation.pdf and attenuation.dat (freq, then psd_open/psd_closed/atten_dB per axis).
pipeline._note3: pixel_scale converts the normalized CoM output to pixels for the report: center_of_mass emits -1..+1 across the whole frame (norm = -1 + 2*px/(dim-1)), so at 56 px wide one x output unit = (56-1)/2 = 27.5 px. If width/height change, update this. CAVEAT: it is a single scalar, so with the non-square 56x32 ROI it is only exact for x; y is really (32-1)/2 = 15.5 px/unit, so reported y px are ~1.8x high. config is a free-text summary of the tuned loop that is printed verbatim on page 2 of the PDF, next to the run's start date/time, the per-10 Hz-band RMS table (in px), and the mean (pointing offset) and RMS-about-mean (jitter) for each axis, open vs closed. Keep config in sync with the pid/kalman/line params below.

params.config:
100, 500, 100, 500, 4096: camera ASI290MM 56x32, exp 50, beam-centred ROI (2026-07-16 setup)
pid: i=1050 iy=1500 + LEAD (s+2pi*8)/(s+2pi*32) both axes -> fc 64/65 Hz, PM 67, model Ms 2.0
(design: grid search over attenuation9 open PSD; lead trades <8 Hz gain for +37 deg at 16-64 Hz)
lines x=[9.6,12.1,21.3,25.8,57.5,83.7] gain=[40,60,60,40,20,15] phase=[41.2,37.6,32.7,31.7,19.1,-3.1] (recentered on the 2026-07-15 line survey)
linesy=[5.6,10.9,21.3,29.8,57.5] gainy=[50,50,40,30,15] phasey=[52.5,39.1,32.8,31.0,19.3]
phases = plant+sensitivity WITH lead: phi=-360*f*tau + angle(1/(1+L))
kalman: order=1024 (was 80; ONLY change vs attn10 -- resolve the 2.26 Hz comb) horizon=5 mu=0.03 gain=1.0 gainy=1.0 ramp=10
piplate: fine ch0(+2)/ch1(-2), coarse bias 1.95/2.02 (2026-07-16 evening setup), baud 460800

pipeline._note: adaptive predictor (see steering_tuned.json). During the open phase it trains on zeros and outputs ~0; settle_time above covers its re-convergence after the loop closes. DO NOT set gain<1: run 4 (attenuation4_kalman07_h6, gain 0.7 + horizon 6) collapsed the x loop -- sensitivity crossover 28->15 Hz, closed/open ratio 0.94 vs 0.60 -- and the bode at the same bias (fsm2310_bode_x_bias105) shows |P|x unchanged, so the blend itself is what broke it (NLMS trains on the blended closed-loop signal and finds a bad equilibrium). gain 1.0 / horizon 5 is the verified best (run 2: x 0.384 px).

params._note2: order 80->1024 (2026-07-15, ONLY change vs attenuation10): the attenuation10 budget shows the 10-30 Hz x residual is a ~2.256 Hz-spaced comb (19.17/21.43/23.68/25.94) and order 80 (35 ms window, ~29 Hz resolution) cannot represent it -- 1024 = fs/2.256, one full beat period of the comb spacing, so the predictor can separate and lock every member. horizon 5 unchanged (delay budget: tau_loop/Ts = 3.93 fr + ~0.5 fr exposure smear + ~0.65 fr DAC ZOH -> ceil = 5). mu 0.03 unchanged; tracking time order/(mu*fs) rises 1.15 s -> 14.8 s (slower than the ~1.7 s wander timescale -- accepted for this A/B; if lines pump or dance, mu is the next knob, NOT gain). ~19 MFLOP/s at 2310 Hz, still negligible. ROLLBACK: order 80.

pipeline._note4: LEAD-COMPENSATED design (2026-07-14 evening, from a grid search of i x lead over the measured attenuation9 open PSD, constraints PM>=45 deg and Ms=max|S|<=2.0, plant Kx=0.427 Ky=0.300 tau=1.81 ms): C(s) = i/s * (s+2pi*8)/(s+2pi*32). The lead's +37 deg near 16-64 Hz buys crossover fc 46->64 Hz (x) and 24->65 Hz (y) at PM 67; its price is 4x less gain below 8 Hz (fz/fp=0.25), which the budget can afford (0-10 Hz closed was 0.050/0.040 px). Predicted from the model: x 10-30 Hz 0.207->0.186, y 10-30 0.246->0.128 px; the model is conservative (ignores oscillators+NLMS, overpredicted attenuation9 by ~25%). i=1050/iy=1500 place |L|=1 at 64/65 Hz WITH the lead's HF gain ~1. ROLLBACK if 60-120 Hz blows up or a line pumps: i=680, iy=500, no lead_* params, and the attenuation9 phase lists (77.8,75.6,63.6,58.7,13.8,-24.3 / 76.5,42.6,26.8,-13.7 at lines 9.8,11.6,21.1,24.9,57.5,83.7 / 5.64,21.1,29.6,57.5).
pipeline._note5: OSCILLATOR PHASES (attenuation8 lesson, VALIDATED by attenuation9: x 0.400->0.282 px, y 0.253->0.221): the default phi=-360*f*line_delay compensates the PLANT only, but the closed-loop command->error path is P*S and phase(S) was +45..+84 deg at these lines -- every notch realized ~0.7 Hz above its oscillator. Correct formula: phi = -360*f*tau + angle(1/(1+L(f))), with L including the LEAD section now (that is why these phases are ~30 deg, not ~70: the lead flattens phase(S) in-band). RECOMPUTE whenever i, iy, lead_*, or bias (K) changes. Line centers on the multi-run measured positions (they wander ~0.5 Hz/run: x 20.9<->22.6, so 21.7 center; y 11.28 is rock-steady across runs and finally gets its own oscillator -- it was y's largest un-notched line at 0.095 px). Gains: notch half-width df=g*K/(4pi), >= the observed wander; cap 20/15 near/above crossover (x 57.5 at 0.90*fc, y 57.5 at 0.88*fc, x 83.3 at 1.3*fc).
pipeline._note6: kalman gainy 1.0 (first y try since 2026-07-10): the old 'prediction pumps y 30.8 Hz' failure happened when fc_y was 62 Hz; y now crosses at 24 Hz and its dominant residual is the predictable 10-30 Hz cluster (0.107 px broadband in 20-30 even with lines notched) plus 57.5 Hz -- exactly what the NLMS eats on x. If this run shows a y line pumped >2x vs attenuation8, set gainy back to 0 (never 0<gainy<1).
